items.superseding_construct: Rust uses Rc<RefCell<T>> with typed singletons in src/model/context.rs; there is no direct emModel base class — the context/registry pattern is replaced by typed fields and ResourceCache
items.notes: The C++ emModel provides ref-counting, context registration (common model lookup), and lifetime management. In Rust, Rc handles ref-counting, and Context (context.rs) handles typed singleton lookup. No 1:1 class.
